title: Telangana Heatwave Likely to Intensify From Saturday - Hyderabad Mail
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Telangana
full_text:
HYDERABAD: Heatwave conditions are likely to intensify across Telangana from Saturday, with the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre warning of rising temperatures, hot winds and humidity in several districts. Temperatures are already crossing 40°C in many parts of the state.
Light Rain Likely in Isolated Areas
According to the weather department, a trough extending from southwest Madhya Pradesh to the Comorin region through Vidarbha, Marathwada, interior Karnataka and Telangana is influencing weather conditions in the state.
Light to moderate rainfall is likely at isolated places in Telangana on Saturday and Sunday. Officials said maximum temperatures are expected to remain largely unchanged over the next three days before increasing further.
Orange Alert Issued for Most Districts
Adilabad, Nirmal, Jagtial, Nizamabad and Komaram Bheem districts are recording high temperatures. The Meteorological Department issued an orange alert for most Telangana districts, excluding the undivided Nalgonda and Khammam districts.
Officials advised elderly people, pregnant women, children and those suffering from chronic illnesses to avoid stepping outdoors during afternoon hours due to severe heat and humidity.
Early Monsoon Relief Expected
The department said scattered pre-monsoon showers may continue in parts of the state. Farmers are facing difficulties due to unseasonal rain in some districts.
Weather officials expect temperatures to reduce during the first week of June with the likely onset of early monsoon activity.

title: Intense heatwave and thunderstorm alert grips Telangana; Scattered rains forecast - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: Amid the intense summer heat, Adilabad sizzled at 44.3 degree Celsius topping the high temperatures list of Telangana on Wednesday.
Day temperatures remained high across most parts of the state with Nizamabad recording 44.2 degree Celsius, Medak logging in 42.6 degree Celsius, Khammam with 42.4 degree Celsius and and Mahabubnagar recording 41.9 degree Celsius.
The grueling heatwave continues to dominate with maximum temperatures soaring past 44 degree celsius in northern districts and 40 degree Celsius in Hyderabad.
While the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad, has forecast isolated thunderstorms, the mercury is expected to remain high in several regions.
Hyderabad remains under orange alert as temperatures hover above 40 degree Celsius. In the last 24 hours, Hayathnagar emerged as the hottest region at 41 degree Celsius followed by Hakimpet (40.8) Rajendra Nagar (40.5), Begumpet (40.4) and Patancheru (39.4).
The IMD, Hyderabad forecast indicated that isolated places in Hyderabad, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Rangareddy, and Sangareddy are likely to experience thunderstorms and gusty winds on Thursday evening or early hours of Friday.
Despite the potential for localized rain, the IMD warns that there will be no large change in maximum temperatures for the next three days. Looking ahead to May 3, the heatwave conditions are projected to intensify further, with temperatures in Hyderabad and surrounding districts expected to range between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.
